hero.title: Improving the Sustainable Management of the
hero.subtitle: Promoting cooperation between countries and strengthening institutional capacity for the protection and sustainable management of the transboundary Senegalo-Mauritanian aquifer system and its dependent ecosystems to improve water and food security and climate change resilience.

about.label: About the Project
about.title: The SMAS Project for
about.description1: The Senegalo-Mauritanian Aquifer System is a vital resource for water supply to the populations of the countries concerned. It supports agriculture, livestock, and local ecosystems, while playing a crucial role in communities' resilience to climate variations.
about.description2: However, it is under increasing pressure from overexploitation, pollution and the impacts of climate change. This project aims to address these challenges by establishing integrated and sustainable management of this precious resource.
about.groundwaterDescription: Coordinated monitoring and protection of shared aquifer resources across 4 nations.

waterSecurity.title: Water Security
waterSecurity.description: Ensuring sustainable access to groundwater resources

transboundary.title: Transboundary Management
transboundary.description: Integrated approach to shared aquifer systems

climateResilience.title: Climate Resilience
climateResilience.description: Building adaptive capacity for future challenges

components.label: Project Components
components.title: Four Key
components.subtitle: A comprehensive approach to managing the Senegal-Mauritanian Aquifer System through knowledge, governance, adaptation, and capacity building.

component1.title: Component 1: Improving Understanding of the Aquifer System
component1.description: Improve the shared understanding of the Senegalo-Mauritanian aquifer system through detailed studies and harmonization of groundwater management tools. It includes regional evaluation and diagnosis of the aquifer system to identify challenges and opportunities related to its sustainable management.

component2.title: Component 2: Development of a Regional Strategic Action Programme (RSAP)
component2.description: Develop and approve an RSAP for the sustainable management of SMAS water resources. The RSAP will include strategies and measures for integrated management of surface and groundwater resources, while taking into account the impacts of climate change.

component3.title: Component 3: Implementation of Groundwater-Based Adaptation Measures
component3.description: Implementation of small-scale pilot measures to demonstrate the effectiveness of improved water resource use. These measures will aim to increase water use efficiency in agriculture and expand water resource availability through adapted local interventions.

component4.title: Component 4: Capacity Building, Communication and Knowledge Management
component4.description: Strengthen institutional and human capacities for SMAS management. Includes the development of a regional information and data exchange platform, training modules, and communication strategies to disseminate project results and increase its visibility.

knowledge.title: Component 1: Aquifer Knowledge
knowledge.description: Improving understanding of the state and functioning of the Senegal-Mauritanian Aquifer System and its interactions with surface waters.

governance.title: Component 2: Regional Governance
governance.description: Developing a regional Strategic Action Program (SAP) and facilitating joint management of surface and groundwater resources.

capacity.title: Component 3 & 4: Capacity Building
capacity.description: Piloting groundwater adaptation solutions and building capacity through training, communication and knowledge management.

partners.title: Our Partners
partners.subtitle: Working together for sustainable water management

introduction.title: Improving Understanding of the Aquifer System
introduction.content: This component aims to improve the shared understanding of the Senegalo-Mauritanian aquifer system through detailed studies and harmonization of groundwater management tools. It includes the regional evaluation and diagnosis of the aquifer system to identify challenges and opportunities related to its sustainable management.

dataCollection.title: Data Collection & Analysis
dataCollection.description: Gathering and analyzing hydrogeological data from across the four countries to build a comprehensive understanding of the aquifer system.

monitoring.title: Monitoring Systems
monitoring.description: Establishing and maintaining monitoring networks to track groundwater levels, quality, and extraction rates in real-time.

interactions.title: River-Aquifer Interactions
interactions.description: Studying the complex relationships between surface water from the Senegal and Gambia rivers and the underlying aquifer system.

mapping.title: Geological Mapping
mapping.description: Creating detailed geological and hydrogeological maps to support resource assessment and management planning.

introduction.title: Regional Strategic Action Programme (RSAP)
introduction.content: The purpose of this component is to develop and approve an RSAP for the sustainable management of SMAS water resources. The RSAP will include strategies and measures for integrated management of surface and groundwater resources, while taking into account the impacts of climate change.

policy.title: Policy Development
policy.description: Developing harmonized policies and regulations for groundwater management across all four countries.

framework.title: Transboundary Framework
framework.description: Creating legal and institutional frameworks to support cooperative management of shared water resources.

governance.title: Governance Mechanisms
governance.description: Establishing governance structures and coordination mechanisms for effective transboundary water management.

planning.title: Joint Planning
planning.description: Developing joint management plans and strategic action programs for sustainable aquifer use.

introduction.title: Groundwater-Based Adaptation Measures
introduction.content: This component focuses on implementing small-scale pilot measures to demonstrate the effectiveness of improved water resource use. These measures will aim to increase water use efficiency in agriculture and expand water resource availability through adapted local interventions.

solutions.title: Adaptation Solutions
solutions.description: Designing and implementing groundwater-based solutions to enhance climate resilience in vulnerable areas.

pilots.title: Pilot Projects
pilots.description: Launching demonstration projects to test and validate adaptation approaches in real-world conditions.

resilience.title: Resilience Building
resilience.description: Strengthening community and ecosystem resilience through targeted interventions and capacity support.

mitigation.title: Risk Mitigation
mitigation.description: Identifying and addressing climate-related risks to water resources and dependent communities.

introduction.title: Capacity Building, Communication and Knowledge Management
introduction.content: This component aims to strengthen institutional and human capacities for SMAS management. It includes the development of a regional information and data exchange platform, training modules, and communication strategies to disseminate project results and increase its visibility.

training.title: Training Programs
training.description: Developing and delivering specialized training for water professionals and decision-makers across the region.

knowledge.title: Knowledge Sharing
knowledge.description: Creating platforms and mechanisms for sharing best practices, data, and lessons learned among stakeholders.

communication.title: Communication Strategy
communication.description: Implementing communication strategies to raise awareness and promote sustainable water management practices.

platform.title: Regional Platform
platform.description: Establishing a regional knowledge management platform for aquifer system information and collaboration.

knowledge.title: Component 1: Aquifer Knowledge
knowledge.subtitle: Improving understanding of the Senegal-Mauritanian Aquifer System

governance.title: Component 2: Regional Governance
governance.subtitle: Developing the regional Strategic Action Program

capacity.subtitle: Climate adaptation solutions and capacity building

reports.subtitle: Access documentation and research outputs from the SMAS project

impact.title: Expected Impact and Beneficiaries
impact.subtitle: The SMAS project (2024-2027) targets concrete results for populations and ecosystems across the four countries

indicators.title: Key Indicators
indicators.protectedAreas: Terrestrial areas protected under improved management: 16,000 hectares
indicators.improvedLandscapes: Area of landscapes under improved practices: 61,213 hectares
indicators.aquaticEcosystems: Shared aquatic ecosystems under improved cooperative management: 1

outcomes.title: Impact
outcomes.waterSecurity: Increased water security in freshwater ecosystems.
outcomes.regionalCooperation: Strengthened regional and national cooperation on shared water resources.
outcomes.climateResilience: Improved climate change resilience for populations dependent on SMAS.

beneficiaries.title: Beneficiaries
beneficiaries.description: The project aims to directly benefit populations in pilot areas through improved water management practices, capacity building and increased cooperation between participating countries.

gender.title: Gender Considerations
gender.description: The project pays particular attention to the gender dimension by integrating specific actions to promote gender equality and women's empowerment. Women, often the most affected by water scarcity and the impacts of climate change, will benefit from training and capacity building programmes to play an active role in water resource management.
